title: Experimental HTTP APIs import server barrel exports that the server explicitly removed
description: packages/server/src/index.ts says getKnowledge/readAgentsMeta are no longer re-exported after agents.meta decolo, but the quarantined HTTP API sources still import readAgentsMeta/getKnowledge from @fenglimg/fabric-server. If this package is restored or typechecked with current server d.ts, those endpoints target a public API that no longer exists.
fix_direction: Either remove/retire the legacy /api/rules and /api/rules/context endpoints, or update them to current store-aware APIs exported by @fenglimg/fabric-server; add a direct typecheck path for the quarantined package before restoration.

issue_history.note: Discovered by prompt inconsistency sweep DBP-20260609-023700

title: Doctor CLI/README still advertise 48 checks after the implementation changed
description: README and doctor CLI help/TLDR hard-code '48 checks', but runDoctorReport currently builds 46 unconditional checks plus one optional promote-ledger check, so a clean report can claim all 48 checks are green even though the implementation no longer has 48 checks in that array.
fix_direction: Stop hard-coding the check count in CLI/docs; derive it from report.checks.length or rewrite copy to avoid an exact number. Update README occurrences and any snapshot expectations together.

context.notes: Discovered by prompt inconsistency sweep DBP-20260609-023700

issue_history.note: Discovered by prompt inconsistency sweep DBP-20260609-023700

title: Tooling manifest protected-token inputs point at obsolete template roots
description: docs/tooling-manifest.json declares lint-protected-tokens inputs as templates/bootstrap/** and templates/claude-skills/**, but the actual script reads packages/cli/templates/bootstrap and packages/cli/templates/skills, and there is no root templates directory. A machine-readable source_of_truth manifest will mislead automation about what the quality gate validates.
fix_direction: Update the manifest paths/names to packages/cli/templates/bootstrap and packages/cli/templates/skills, or explicitly mark bootstrap as absent and covered by packages/shared/src/templates/bootstrap-canonical.ts tests.

context.notes: Discovered by prompt inconsistency sweep DBP-20260609-023700

issue_history.note: Discovered by prompt inconsistency sweep DBP-20260609-023700

title: Published server package metadata still describes retired .fabric/agents.meta storage
description: @fenglimg/fabric-server package.json describes the server as managing a .fabric knowledge base plus agents.meta.json, while current project policy says knowledge entries live only in mounted stores and server index comments say the co-location agents.meta surface is no longer re-exported. This stale npm metadata presents the old storage model to package consumers.
fix_direction: Refresh the package description to mention mounted stores, stdio MCP, event ledger/metrics, and store-aware knowledge retrieval; avoid advertising .fabric/knowledge or agents.meta.json as managed storage.

context.notes: Discovered by prompt inconsistency sweep DBP-20260609-023700

issue_history.note: Discovered by prompt inconsistency sweep DBP-20260609-023700

title: Store-aware MCP contract advertises provenance and written_to_store fields that runtime outputs do not produce
description: `MCP_STORE_AWARE_CONTRACTS` and its tests lock a 6-tool store-aware contract where READ tools surface `global_ref`/`provenance` and WRITE tools echo `written_to_store`, and the parity matrix describes recall/sections as returning `store_uuid + alias + local_id + global_ref`. Actual output schemas/services are incompatible: `knowledgeSectionsOutputSchema.rules[]` only has `stable_id/level/path/body`, `recallOutputSchema.rules[]` only adds optional `store:{alias}`, and `FabExtractKnowledgeOutputSchema`/`extractKnowledge` return only `pending_path` and `idempotency_key`. Clients relying on the exported contract can believe store-qualified citation/write-target data exists while MCP responses omit it, especially under multi-store shadowing.
fix_direction: Either make the real MCP output schemas and services emit the promised store edge (`global_ref`, `provenance`, and `written_to_store`) for all contracted tools, or narrow `MCP_STORE_AWARE_CONTRACTS`/parity descriptions/tests to the fields actually shipped; add contract tests that validate runtime tool outputs against the exported store-aware contract rather than only validating standalone helper schemas.

context.notes: Discovered by prompt inconsistency sweep DBP-20260609-023700

issue_history.note: Discovered by prompt inconsistency sweep DBP-20260609-023700

title: Project route-write is ignored by archive/review write-target resolution
description: Invariant: after `fabric store route-write project:fabric-v2 platform`, a team-layer archive in a repo bound to `active_project=fabric-v2` should land in the routed `platform` store and record matching visibility. The shared resolver supports project routes (`packages/shared/test/store/privacy-boundary.test.ts:55`), and the CLI persists routes (`packages/cli/src/store/store-ops.ts:476`), but server write-target helpers always resolve the literal `team` scope for team-layer writes (`packages/server/src/services/cross-store-write.ts:60`, `packages/server/src/services/cross-store-write.ts:115`) before later stamping `semantic_scope: project:<active_project>` (`packages/server/src/services/cross-store-write.ts:123`). `extractKnowledge` and review promotion/layer-flip use those helpers (`packages/server/src/services/extract-knowledge.ts:359`, `packages/server/src/services/review.ts:607`, `packages/server/src/services/review.ts:964`), so the config write round-trip silently falls back to the default/active team store instead of the route.
fix_direction: Derive an effective write scope once (`personal` for personal, otherwise `project:${active_project}` when present, else `team`) and pass that scope into `resolveWriteTarget` for both target dir and visibility metadata. Add an integration test that seeds two writable stores, sets `write_routes: [{scope:'project:fabric-v2', store:'platform'}]` plus `active_project:'fabric-v2'`, runs `extractKnowledge` and review approve, then asserts pending/canonical paths, counters, and `visibility_store` land in `platform`.

context.notes: Discovered by prompt inconsistency sweep DBP-20260609-023700

issue_history.note: Discovered by prompt inconsistency sweep DBP-20260609-023700

title: fab_recall include_related only works with store-qualified related IDs despite bare-id contract
description: Invariant: a frontmatter edge authored as `related: [KT-GLD-0001]` should round-trip through plan candidates and `fab_recall({include_related:true})` to fetch that neighbor. The schema/builder document and parse bare stable ids (`packages/shared/src/schemas/agents-meta.ts:80`, `packages/server/src/services/knowledge-meta-builder.ts:1070`, `packages/server/src/services/knowledge-meta-builder.ts:1245`), while cross-store recall emits store-qualified candidate ids via `entry.qualifiedId` (`packages/server/src/services/cross-store-recall.ts:163`). `recall` compares `candidate.description.related` directly against store-qualified `candidateIds` (`packages/server/src/services/recall.ts:132`, `packages/server/src/services/recall.ts:137`, `packages/server/src/services/recall.ts:148`), and the test only passes because it seeds a non-contract `related: [team:KT-GLD-0001]` (`packages/server/src/services/recall.test.ts:196`). Real bare related edges therefore neither expand nor produce the next-step nudge.
fix_direction: Normalize related ids before comparison: accept bare local ids by qualifying them with the source candidate's store alias when possible, or compare against both local and qualified id maps. Update recall tests to seed `related: [KT-GLD-0001]` and add cross-store ambiguity coverage so include_related behavior is deterministic.

context.notes: Discovered by prompt inconsistency sweep DBP-20260609-023700

issue_history.note: Discovered by prompt inconsistency sweep DBP-20260609-023700

title: In-flight drain uses a single waiter slot, so concurrent shutdown drains can overwrite each other
description: createShutdownHandler is per-signal, so SIGTERM and SIGINT handlers can both enter the first-invocation path and call tracker.drain concurrently. The tracker stores only one resolveDrained callback; the later drain overwrites the earlier one, so when active requests exit only the newest waiter resolves immediately while the earlier shutdown path waits until its deadline and may then run fsync/flush/close/exit out of phase with the other handler.
fix_direction: Represent drain waiters as a Set/array and resolve all pending waiters when active.size reaches 0, or centralize shutdown dedup across all signals so only one drain can be active; add a regression test for concurrent drain() calls before the final exit().

context.notes: Discovered by prompt inconsistency sweep DBP-20260609-023700

issue_history.note: Discovered by prompt inconsistency sweep DBP-20260609-023700

title: Metrics flush window is global mutable state shared across project roots
description: startMetricsFlush stores each root's timer in flushTimers, but also writes the selected interval to the module-level flushIntervalMs. flushMetrics later labels rows with options.windowMs ?? flushIntervalMs, so starting a second projectRoot with a different interval changes the window label used by manual/final flushes for every other project even though counters are per-project.
fix_direction: Store interval/window metadata per projectRoot alongside each timer, or pass the captured interval into every timer and stop-handle flush; add a test that starts two roots with different intervals and verifies each emitted row keeps its own window.

context.notes: Discovered by prompt inconsistency sweep DBP-20260609-023700

issue_history.note: Discovered by prompt inconsistency sweep DBP-20260609-023700

title: CI perf benchmark uses a legacy minimal fixture instead of the mounted-store workflow
description: The CI perf gate claims to measure interactive `fabric doctor` and SessionStart cold-start latency, but setupCliFixture only creates `.fabric/agents.meta.json` and `.fabric/events.jsonl` in a synthetic temp root. It never creates a current mounted store, store.json, project binding, or resolved-bindings snapshot, so store resolver / read-set / bindings regressions can pass this p95 gate while production-shaped projects slow down.
fix_direction: Add a second production-shaped benchmark fixture that creates a Fabric home, mounted store with store.json and knowledge entries, project fabric-config binding, and bindings snapshot; gate or at least report p95 for that mounted-store doctor/hook path from CI.

context.notes: Discovered by prompt inconsistency sweep DBP-20260609-023700

issue_history.note: Discovered by prompt inconsistency sweep DBP-20260609-023700

title: Exposed rc6 gate is stale against current hook/config files
description: The root package still exposes `pnpm rc6:gate`, but the gate re-runs a stale rc5 anchor and checks current files against outdated assumptions. With build/tests skipped, it fails on rc5 legacy checks, missing Cursor SessionStart/PreToolUse slots, and a deleted `packages/cli/src/commands/hooks.ts` path before it can provide a useful release signal. This makes the named gate misleading and likely to be bypassed rather than trusted.
fix_direction: Either retire rc5:gate/rc6:gate from package.json, or update rc6 checks to current hook config ownership and command files, then add a lightweight test/CI assertion that exposed gate scripts execute successfully with FABRIC_GATE_SKIP_BUILD=1 and FABRIC_GATE_SKIP_TESTS=1.
